title: Representative Eval Sets, Deterministic Oracles, Baselines, and Versions
description: The team tests five hand-picked happy prompts, changes them after failures, has no task distribution or deterministic checks, and compares only final prose. Learners cumulatively produce versioned representative agent evaluation corpus through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Sample normal, boundary, ambiguous, multilingual, accessibility, refusal, tool, failure, adversarial, and recovery cases from the intended task distribution.
Define exact schema, state, tool, argument, authorization, effect, citation, budget, stop, and recovery assertions wherever outcomes are deterministic.
Create changed-input, changed-policy, tool-fault, injection, memory-poisoning, approval, cancellation, and resource-exhaustion cases without leaking answers.
